en: I don't know how you do things, Yura, yet.
ko: 아직 너가 어떻게 하는지 모르겠어, 유라.

en: You see, I've spent most of my life in Europe.
You shouldn't use words like that. Says who?
ko: 그런 말은 쓰지 말아야 해. 누가 그래?

en: Oh, Meg, she's my older sister. Here.
ko: 오, 메그, 그녀는 내 언니야. 여기.

en: Oh, that's her, see? The girl in the violet dress? Mm, it's very pretty.
ko: 저기, 보이지? 보라색 드레스를 입은 여자애. 음, 정말 예쁘다.

en: She reminds me to be good,
so father will be proud of me when he returns.
He volunteered for the Union Army,
and I wanted to go fight with him.
I can't get over my disappointment in being a girl.
ko: 여자라는 사실에 실망을 극복할 수 없어.

en: Jo, would you like to dance with me?
I can't because... Because of what?
You won't tell? Never. I scorched my dress, see?
ko: 말 안 할 거지? 절대. 드레스를 태웠어, 봐?

en: There, and Meg told me to keep still so no one would see it.
ko: 저기, 메그가 아무도 보지 않게 가만히 있으라고 했어.

en: You can laugh if you want to, it's funny, I know.
I have an idea about that.
